level_signals: Comp range $290K-$365K base, consistent with Senior IC at Anthropic
Owns cross-team operational cadences and platform migrations independently
Expected to establish SLOs and incident processes with partner teams without direct authority

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have solid technical program management experience, particularly in operational or infrastructure-heavy environments — you're comfortable owning a mix of ongoing operational cadences and discrete project work simultaneously.

Understand how production ML systems work well enough to triage incidents intelligently and have substantive conversations with engineers about what's going wrong and why — you don't need to write the code, but you need to follow the technical thread.

Are energized by closing loops. Post-mortem action items that never get done, SLOs that no one checks, runbooks that go stale — these things bother you, and you know how to build the processes and follow-ups that fix them.

Can work effectively across team boundaries — comfortable coordinating with partner teams (like Inference) where you don't have direct authority, and skilled at keeping shared work moving through influence and clear communication.

Thrive in environments where the work shifts between "keep the lights on" and "build something new" — and can context-switch between incident follow-ups and longer-horizon platform projects without dropping either.

Have experience with or strong interest in AI safety — you understand why the reliability of a safety-critical pipeline is a different kind of problem than the reliability of a product feature, and that distinction motivates you.
Minimum education: Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have experience with SRE practices, incident management frameworks, or on-call operations at scale.
Have worked on or with evaluation infrastructure for ML systems — understanding how evals get designed, run, and interpreted.
Have experience driving infrastructure migrations in complex, multi-team environments — particularly where the migration touches operational systems that can't go offline.
Be familiar with monitoring and alerting tooling (PagerDuty, Datadog, or equivalents) and the operational culture around them.

scope.surface: Safeguards Engineering infrastructure — classifiers, detection pipelines, evaluation platforms, and monitoring systems for AI safety in production
primary_metrics_inferred: Incident post-mortem action item closure rate
core_responsibilities: Own the Safeguards Engineering ops review cadence
Drive incident tracking and post-mortem execution, ensuring action items are closed
Establish and maintain SLOs with partner teams (Inference, Cloud Inference)
Maintain runbook quality and incident-ownership clarity
Drive platform migrations and infrastructure projects (incident platforms, cloud monitoring)
Coordinate evals platform improvements including self-serve capabilities and eval factory infrastructure

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($290K-$365K base)
Visa sponsorship explicitly offered with good-faith effort commitment and immigration lawyer retained
Clear and specific role surface — not vague; detailed responsibilities around incident management, SLOs, and migrations
Encourages applications from candidates who don't meet every qualification
No application deadline — rolling basis reduces urgency pressure
Role has high organizational leverage in a safety-critical domain
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires at least 25% in-office time; San Francisco-based — eliminates fully remote candidates
Minimum years of experience not stated numerically; correlated to internal level which is opaque to candidates
Role touches sensitive safety systems (CSAM detection, account-banning) which may carry unusual operational and ethical weight
Compensation band is wide ($75K spread) which may indicate ambiguity in leveling

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and independence of responsibilities; not stated explicitly)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly listed in JD)
location.cities_listed (only San Francisco mentioned as HQ; no other cities named for this role)
posting_first_seen and posting_last_updated (not available in source)
ambiguities: Years of experience minimum is not stated numerically; JD says 'Years of experience required will correlate with the internal job level requirements for the position' — level is not disclosed to candidates.

The 'Have experience with or strong interest in AI safety' requirement uses 'or strong interest', making it partially bridgeable, but it is listed under the 'You might be a good fit if' unconditional section — treated as hard with bridgeable=yes.
'Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience' could be read as a soft requirement given the equivalency clause; classified as soft.
